text.title: Modern Python Tooling Stack
text.summary: uv commands, ruff config, pyright/mypy alignment, and hatchling build backend for 2026 Python projects.

section.title: uv — Single Tool for Python Management

rule.statement: Use `uv` for Python version management, virtual environments, and package management. Do not mix uv with pip, venv, pyenv, or poetry in the same project.
rule.rationale: uv is 10-100x faster than pip (warm cache), manages Python versions itself, and uses a global cache. Running pip alongside uv produces environment state divergence — the lockfile (`uv.lock`) becomes unreliable.

rule.statement: Commit `uv.lock` to version control. Run `uv sync --frozen` in CI to install exactly what the lockfile specifies.
rule.rationale: `uv.lock` is the reproducibility contract. `--frozen` fails if the lockfile is stale, catching accidental `uv add` commits that weren't also followed by `uv lock`. This prevents "works in CI, breaks in prod" dependency drift.

example.note: Essential uv commands for daily project work
example.code:
# Init uv init my-project --python 3.12 uv python pin 3.12 # Creates .python-version
# Deps uv add fastapi pydantic uv add --dev pytest ruff mypy pytest-cov
# Run (no venv activation needed) uv run python main.py uv run pytest --cov=src uv run ruff check . --fix && uv run ruff format .
# CI uv sync --frozen # Install exact lockfile state

section.reference: Full project bootstrap script using uv

rule.statement: Enable ruff rule groups: `E F I B UP N S C4 PT`. Run `ruff check --fix` before commit and `ruff format` in pre-commit hooks. Use `target-version` matching `requires-python`.
rule.rationale: Ruff replaces Black + Flake8 + isort + pyupgrade in a single millisecond-speed pass. `UP` auto-upgrades deprecated typing syntax. `S` catches security-relevant patterns (hardcoded secrets, subprocess shell=True). `PT` enforces pytest style.

example.code:
[tool.ruff] line-length = 100 target-version = "py312"
[tool.ruff.lint] select = ["E", "F", "I", "B", "UP", "N", "S", "C4", "PT", "T20"] ignore = ["S101"] # assert allowed in tests
[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores] "tests/**" = ["S"] # Security rules relaxed in tests
[tool.ruff.format] quote-style = "double"

section.title: hatchling Build Backend

rule.statement: Use `hatchling` as the build backend for new libraries and distributable packages. For application-only projects (no PyPI distribution), the build backend can be omitted.
rule.rationale: hatchling is the PEP 517/518 build backend recommended by the Python Packaging Authority (PyPA). It replaces setuptools for new projects and integrates cleanly with uv. It requires no `setup.py` or `MANIFEST.in`.

section.reference: Complete pyproject.toml with hatchling, ruff, mypy, pytest

rule.statement: Keep `requires-python`, ruff `target-version`, mypy `python_version`, and pyright `pythonVersion` in sync. All four must reference the same minor version.
rule.rationale: Misalignment produces false CI passes: ruff may not flag syntax errors from a higher version if `target-version` is too low; mypy may allow features not available on the deployment Python if `python_version` is too high. Sync prevents silent version drift.
